cases.name: pytest_python_support_base_value

cases.name: pytest_python_ceiling_base_value
cases.answer: At that point, Python 3.13 was the newest explicitly supported Python line in the changelog. pytest 8.4.0 added support for Python 3.13, described as support for Python 3.13 beta1 at the time of writing.

cases.name: fastapi_pydantic_migration_base_value
cases.answer: FastAPI 0.119.0 supports both Pydantic v2 models and pydantic.v1 models at the same time in the same app to ease migration. FastAPI Pydantic migration before the cleanup.

cases.name: typer_python_support_base_value
cases.answer: Before the later breaking cleanup, Typer still supported Python 3.9+, while the release stream was already upgrading types for Python 3.10 and adding internal scripts to migrate docs from Python 3.9 to Python 3.10.

cases.name: httpx_app_shortcut_base_value
cases.answer: In HTTPX 0.27.0, the `app=...` shortcut was still present but deprecated.

cases.name: httpx_proxy_cleanup_base_value
cases.answer: The old proxies argument would still continue to work for the moment, even though HTTPX 0.26.0 added the replacement proxy argument and deprecated proxies. The documented path was already to use proxy or mounts for more complex configurations.

cases.name: httpx_app_shortcut_updated_value
cases.answer: In HTTPX 0.28.0, the `app` argument has now been removed. The explicit transport style with `transport=httpx.WSGITransport()` or `transport=httpx.ASGITransport()` remained the documented path after the removal.

cases.name: httpx_proxy_cleanup_updated_value
cases.answer: HTTPX 0.28.0 says the deprecated proxies argument has now been removed. The same official release stream still points users to the replacement proxy argument, or mounts for more complex configurations.
